intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. The pots contain minimal to no visible foliage, suggesting a state of near-total systemic loss or dormancy.

inventory_reconciliation.P1 (Yellow Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible String of Nickels foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P2 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible Mexican Mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.P3 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: White rabbit anchor present, but no Pothos foliage visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4 (Black Pot): Systemic Loss: No visible Silver Guest foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2/P4: Soil appears dark and potentially over-saturated; no identifiable plant matter.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine remains; a single, thin, green sprout (likely a weed or adventitious growth) is visible in the foreground pot.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White granular material (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) present on the surface of the foreground pot.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-3: No significant change; pots appear dormant.
temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Minor shift in light reflection; no biological growth.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Emergence of a single, thin green sprout in the foreground pot.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Sprout remains static; no further development.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of established foliage and the presence of only a single, unidentifiable sprout suggests the original specimens have perished or entered a terminal state of decay.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, thin green sprout in the foreground pot is an 'Incidental Growth' not matching the registry. The absence of all registered plants is the primary anomaly.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently devoid of its intended occupants. The pots contain only soil and, in one instance, a small, unidentified sprout. The white rabbit anchor remains as the only constant. The environment appears to have suffered a total loss of the registered species, likely due to environmental stress or neglect.
